commentary_text: The market assigns Brazil a 56% win probability versus the model's 38%, a 18-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. Sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices align closely, suggesting real-money flows are concentrated on Brazil's side. The model sees Morocco as a plausible 30% winner; the market cuts that to 18%. This confidence gap could reflect recent form, roster changes, or injury status that the model has not yet incorporated. The dashboard flags this as a signal worth monitoring—sharper conviction on Brazil's chances may rest on information the statistical baseline does not capture.
